title: The Gang F***s Up Abbott Elementary

jokes.text: I can't believe these shitheads forgot they were mic'd.
jokes.setup: The gang has been scheming privately while wearing microphones
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment to camera that they're idiots who forgot about recording equipment
jokes.explanation: Strong meta-comedy that breaks fourth wall while staying in character. Economical and perfectly timed revelation.

dialogue.line: I can't believe these shitheads forgot they were mic'd.

jokes.text: How is a school a gold mine? - Copper piping. Thousands and thousands of yards of it.
jokes.setup: Frank sees opportunity at elementary school
jokes.punchline: The 'opportunity' is literally stealing infrastructure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic - seeing criminal opportunity in innocent setting. Well-structured reveal of his twisted thinking.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: How is a school a gold mine?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Copper piping. Thousands and thousands of yards of it.

jokes.text: Did you guys know that Black people can't get lice? - Dee, that's, like, crazy racist. - No, no, no. That's just science. It's the lice who are racist.
jokes.setup: Dee makes racist statement about lice
jokes.punchline: Doubles down by claiming the lice themselves are racist
jokes.explanation: Classic Sunny absurdist logic - deflecting racism accusation with even more absurd claim. Character-perfect for Dee.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Did you guys know that Black people can't get lice?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dee, that's, like, crazy racist.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: No, no, no. That's just science. It's the lice who are racist.

jokes.text: Mmm. I need to say a bad word. - It's okay. Yeah. - Yeah? - I'm gonna. It's real bad. - How much of a total fucking cunt she was.
jokes.setup: Sweet teacher character building up to saying 'a bad word'
jokes.punchline: Uses the most shocking profanity possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and escalation. The prolonged buildup makes the payoff devastating. Subverts character expectations brilliantly.

dialogue.speaker: Janine
dialogue.line: Mmm. I need to say a bad word.

dialogue.speaker: Camera crew
dialogue.line: It's okay.

dialogue.speaker: Janine
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Camera crew
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Janine
dialogue.line: I'm gonna. It's real bad.

dialogue.speaker: Camera crew
dialogue.line: How much of a total fucking cunt she was.

jokes.text: I thought you was gonna call her a bitch. - No. I didn't feel that was strong enough. - Bitch can be a very fine dog. That is not what this woman is.
jokes.setup: Following up the shocking profanity
jokes.punchline: Janine calmly explains her word choice with dog comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant follow-up that maintains the shock while adding logical justification. The dog comparison is unexpectedly thoughtful and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Camera operator
dialogue.line: I thought you was gonna call her a bitch.

dialogue.speaker: Janine
dialogue.line: No. I didn't feel that was strong enough. - Bitch can be a very fine dog. That is not what this woman is.

jokes.text: You. You. - You. - No, you. - It's definitely you. Right now. One, two, three. You.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis trying to determine who should leave
jokes.punchline: Childish pointing game with countdown
jokes.explanation: Effective physical comedy showing their immaturity, though relatively simple execution.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You. You.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, you.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's definitely you.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Right now. One, two, three. You.

jokes.text: I'll steal her man. I'll expose her for the dud she is. What do you think? Let's work together.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to manipulate the documentary crew
jokes.punchline: Openly plotting to sabotage Janine on camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee behavior - narcissistic and tone-deaf to being filmed while plotting.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'll steal her man. I'll expose her for the dud she is. What do you think? Let's work together.

jokes.text: Hey, dude. Pfft. This place is the worst, man. They're, like, totally, like, trying to teach me to read and stuff.
jokes.setup: Mac talking to elementary school student
jokes.punchline: Mac complaining about being taught to read like it's unreasonable
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Mac's intellectual insecurity and inappropriate relationship with children.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hey, dude. Pfft. This place is the worst, man. They're, like, totally, like, trying to teach me to read and stuff.

jokes.text: I'm like Walter White over here.
jokes.setup: Charlie compares his coffee making to something impressive
jokes.punchline: He compares making coffee to cooking meth
jokes.explanation: Charlie's pop culture reference is hilariously inappropriate - comparing coffee to meth production shows his complete lack of perspective.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm like Walter White over here.

jokes.text: Yeah. I bought a racoon in to keep that janitor busy.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his distraction strategy
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of releasing wild animal in school
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical Frank chaos - maximum disruption with casual delivery. Original and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah. I bought a racoon in to keep that janitor busy.

jokes.text: Are you pissing in that locker? - Oh, it's the coffee. I drank so much coffee. - It goes right through you, yeah.
jokes.setup: Teacher catches inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually admits to urinating in school locker
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie gross-out humor with matter-of-fact delivery. Effective cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Are you pissing in that locker?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, it's the coffee. I drank so much coffee.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It goes right through you, yeah.

jokes.text: I think white people aren't supposed to help Black people anymore. Right? - No. That's a misunderstanding. - St. Joe's is predominantly white teams, so we're helping the basketball team. We're helping a white school, so it's fine.
jokes.setup: Mac's confused understanding of racial sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Dennis's twisted logic making exploitation okay
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of racial dynamics with characteristically twisted Sunny logic.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I think white people aren't supposed to help Black people anymore. Right?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No. That's a misunderstanding.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: St. Joe's is predominantly white teams, so we're helping the basketball team. We're helping a white school, so it's fine.

jokes.text: St. Joe's is predominantly white teams, so we're helping the basketball team. We're helping a white school, so it's fine.
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to justify their recruitment plan
jokes.punchline: He thinks it's okay because they're helping a white school
jokes.explanation: Dennis's twisted logic to justify exploitation by making it about helping white people instead. The mental gymnastics are perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: St. Joe's is predominantly white teams, so we're helping the basketball team. We're helping a white school, so it's fine.

jokes.text: What's it gonna take to convince you to leave a place like this and come with us? We may have access to a Lexus if you wanna joyride. - You like joyriding? - Vroom, vroom.
jokes.setup: Trying to recruit student for basketball
jokes.punchline: Offering minor a stolen car with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe as they cross multiple legal lines with childish car sound effects.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What's it gonna take to convince you to leave a place like this and come with us? We may have access to a Lexus if you wanna joyride.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You like joyriding?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Vroom, vroom.

jokes.text: Don't think we didn't try. He's trash. - A basketball recruit? Oh, thank God. My mind was going to terrible places.
jokes.setup: Teacher's relief about basketball recruitment
jokes.punchline: Implication that worse scenarios were considered
jokes.explanation: Dark implication humor that lets audience fill in disturbing blanks.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Don't think we didn't try. He's trash.

dialogue.speaker: Jacob
dialogue.line: A basketball recruit? Oh, thank God. My mind was going to terrible places.

jokes.text: The kids don't know anything about it, man. - They don't know what it is. - I heard a couple of 'em joking, and they were sharing funny, like, Internet memes. And they were, like, 'Oh, yeah. Oh, that? I know that Internet joke thing.'
jokes.setup: Charlie upset about kids' 9/11 knowledge
jokes.punchline: Kids only know 9/11 as internet memes
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about generational differences and internet culture.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The kids don't know anything about it, man.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: They don't know what it is.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I heard a couple of 'em joking, and they were sharing funny, like, Internet memes. And they were, like, 'Oh, yeah. Oh, that? I know that Internet joke thing.'

jokes.text: It's 9/11, man. - What-What-What? - What about 9/11? The kids don't know anything about it, man.
jokes.setup: Charlie is upset about education
jokes.punchline: His concern is specifically about 9/11 knowledge
jokes.explanation: Charlie's specific obsession with 9/11 education is random but perfectly in character for his conspiracy mindset.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's 9/11, man.
What-What-What?
What about 9/11?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The kids don't know anything about it, man.

jokes.text: Golf's not for sale, dude. The purest sport in the history of humanity? What's next, football?
jokes.setup: Frank mentions Arabs buying golf
jokes.punchline: Mac treats golf as if it's literally being sold as a sport
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist misunderstanding with Mac's literal interpretation and golf obsession.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Golf's not for sale, dude. The purest sport in the history of humanity? What's next, football?

jokes.text: What's next, football? - The purest sport in the history of humanity? What happened to 'never forget'?
jokes.setup: Discussing foreign influence on American sports
jokes.punchline: Frank treating golf as sacred while mixing up 9/11 messaging
jokes.explanation: Frank's misplaced priorities and confused patriotism create mild absurdist humor.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What's next, football?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The purest sport in the history of humanity? What happened to 'never forget'?

jokes.text: I mean, steel would not melt at that temperature. - You try it. - A missile sent by our own government is probably what it was. - I'm starting to think there wasn't even towers, dude. - Has anybody seen the towers? With your own eyes, not a picture.
jokes.setup: Gang discussing 9/11 conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: Escalating from physics to complete denial of towers' existence
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation showing how conspiracy thinking spirals into complete absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I mean, steel would not melt at that temperature.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You try it.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: A missile sent by our own government is probably what it was.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm starting to think there wasn't even towers, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Has anybody seen the towers? With your own eyes, not a picture.

jokes.text: They control the weather. - Yeah. With frogs. I saw that. - They shoot frogs up there? - They're throwing tornadoes at Florida. - Yeah. It creates a super storm. - The oranges. There's too many oranges.
jokes.setup: Weather control conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: Bizarre frog-based weather control ending with orange surplus concern
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd conspiracy escalation with the orange concern being unexpectedly specific and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: They control the weather.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: With frogs. I saw that.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: They shoot frogs up there?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: They're throwing tornadoes at Florida.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah. It creates a super storm.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The oranges. There's too many oranges.

jokes.text: My main concern is he's coming off like kind of a nerd. You know we've already got our nerd. - We need a bad boy. - Hey, Steven, would you say you're a bad boy? - I don't know. - You don't know? I feel like a bad boy would know if he was bad, right?
jokes.setup: Auditioning kids for boy band roles
jokes.punchline: Asking elementary student to self-identify as 'bad boy'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist premise of applying adult entertainment industry logic to children.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: My main concern is he's coming off like kind of a nerd. You know we've already got our nerd. - We need a bad boy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Hey, Steven, would you say you're a bad boy?

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You don't know? I feel like a bad boy would know if he was bad, right?

jokes.text: We got the ugly one that people feel sorry for, but we... - He's so ugly too. But he is uglier than... - Maybe too ugly.
jokes.setup: Categorizing children for boy band
jokes.punchline: Casually discussing child's ugliness with escalating cruelty
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy showing their complete lack of empathy, though relies on shock value.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We got the ugly one that people feel sorry for, but we...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's so ugly too.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: But he is uglier than...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Maybe too ugly.

jokes.text: Because the bad boy makes the sexual component less weird.
jokes.setup: Explaining boy band dynamics
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of sexual component in children's group
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark and economical line that reveals disturbing thought process with matter-of-fact delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because the bad boy makes the sexual component less weird.

jokes.text: Shit. I might frame 'em for another crime just to get their hours up.
jokes.setup: Ava wants to keep the gang as volunteers
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of willingness to frame them
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Ava's pragmatic amorality with perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: Ava
dialogue.line: Shit. I might frame 'em for another crime just to get their hours up.

jokes.text: I let that bitch kick my ass so that I could look like a victim. You think I couldn't kick her... She's tiny. I could destroy her. I'd rip her hair out. I'd rip this whole school bald if I wanted to.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining fight strategy
jokes.punchline: Escalating to threats against entire school population
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee escalation showing her violent fantasies and persecution complex.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I let that bitch kick my ass so that I could look like a victim. You think I couldn't kick her... She's tiny. I could destroy her. I'd rip her hair out. I'd rip this whole school bald if I wanted to.

jokes.text: I'm so sorry. I saw a mosquito.
jokes.setup: Janine trying to appear calm after violent outburst
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse for clearly aggressive behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to earlier violence with perfect deadpan delivery and obvious lie.

dialogue.speaker: Janine
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry. I saw a mosquito.

jokes.text: On the subject of Billy Joel with the scale being, one: 'I can't stand his music' to ten: 'I think he's the single greatest musician in the history of sound,' where do you place the man? - Negative five.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks for Billy Joel rating on a 1-10 scale
jokes.punchline: She goes below the scale to negative five
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the expected scale. Dennis's elaborate setup makes the dismissive response funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: On the subject of Billy Joel with the scale being, one: 'I can't stand his music' to ten: 'I think he's the single greatest musician in the history of sound,' where do you place the man?

dialogue.speaker: Melissa
dialogue.line: Negative five.

jokes.text: Negative five. But you know who is a massive fan? - A hundred fifty. A million ten. Infinity.
jokes.setup: Rating Billy Joel fandom on 1-10 scale
jokes.punchline: Dennis breaking mathematics with enthusiasm levels
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's tendency to extremes and poor understanding of scales.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Negative five.

dialogue.speaker: Melissa
dialogue.line: But you know who is a massive fan?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: A hundred fifty. A million ten. Infinity.

jokes.text: ♪ Price of copper Is on the rise ♪
jokes.setup: Updated 'We Didn't Start the Fire' lyrics
jokes.punchline: Including Frank's copper pipe obsession in historical song
jokes.explanation: Clever callback weaving Frank's specific criminal interest into mock-historical song format.

dialogue.line: ♪ Price of copper Is on the rise ♪

jokes.text: You're telling me that an entire building is gonna melt to the ground but we can't even melt the bench? - Oh! Oh, shit. It's going up the wall.
jokes.setup: Testing 9/11 conspiracy theory with fire
jokes.punchline: Immediately proving themselves wrong by starting real fire
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic comedy - their conspiracy test immediately backfires in obvious way.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You're telling me that an entire building is gonna melt to the ground but we can't even melt the bench?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh, shit. It's going up the wall.

jokes.text: Will you excuse us for a moment? We're gonna need a... We're just gonna need a minute. - Is it to go listen to their version? - No.
jokes.setup: Gang learning Fall Out Boy already updated the song
jokes.punchline: Teacher's innocent question hitting their exact insecurity
jokes.explanation: Well-timed cringe moment where outsider innocently exposes their embarrassment.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Will you excuse us for a moment? We're gonna need a... We're just gonna need a minute.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Is it to go listen to their version?

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Well then, no this is Billy Joel's song. - No, no. The band Fall Out Boy, they already did an update... - Like, of this song. Changed all the lyrics and... - Who's Fall Out Boy?
jokes.setup: The gang thinks they had an original idea
jokes.punchline: Fall Out Boy already did the same thing, and Charlie doesn't know who they are
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of their pride and Charlie's out-of-touch character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well then, no this is Billy Joel's song.

dialogue.speaker: Janine
dialogue.line: No, no. The band Fall Out Boy, they already did an update...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Like, of this song. Changed all the lyrics and...

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Who's Fall Out Boy?

jokes.text: Son of a bitch! Every goddamn time we try a single thing this goddamn Fall Out Boy! - Always a step ahead of us! Fuckers! - Plus the eyeliner! That was my look. - It was your look, Mac! That has been your look!
jokes.setup: Gang's fury at being upstaged by Fall Out Boy
jokes.punchline: Mac's claim that pop-punk eyeliner was originally his look
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Mac's delusional self-image and the gang's petty rivalries.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Son of a bitch! Every goddamn time we try a single thing this goddamn Fall Out Boy!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Always a step ahead of us! Fuckers!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Plus the eyeliner! That was my look.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It was your look, Mac! That has been your look!

jokes.text: It reminded me of that verse on DMX's 'What You Want.' 'There was Brenda, LaTisha, Linda, Felicia.' I wasn't mad at it. It was a bop.
jokes.setup: Ava reviewing the gang's video
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated musical analysis ending with 'it was a bop'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ava's unexpected musical knowledge and casual assessment style.

dialogue.speaker: Ava
dialogue.line: It reminded me of that verse on DMX's 'What You Want.' 'There was Brenda, LaTisha, Linda, Felicia.' I wasn't mad at it. It was a bop.

jokes.text: Chardonnay, please. - Gosh, we don't have a bottle already open. - Heineken. - Well, no imports though. - Jesus Christ. How about an iced tea? - I think we have ice. - Nope. You don't have ice. - We're off water altogether.
jokes.setup: Gang trying to order drinks at terrible bar
jokes.punchline: Bar somehow doesn't have any basic drink components including water
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation comedy showing the bar's complete dysfunction through drink ordering.

dialogue.line: Chardonnay, please.
Gosh, we don't have a bottle already open.
Heineken.
Well, no imports though.
Jesus Christ. How about an iced tea?
I think we have ice.
Nope. You don't have ice.
We're off water altogether.

jokes.text: You blindsided us with your heart
jokes.setup: Dennis giving seemingly sincere speech about teachers
jokes.punchline: Using 'blindsided' inappropriately while praising them
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's inability to express genuine emotion without violent metaphors.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You blindsided us with your heart

jokes.text: He must've ripped out about a hundred feet of copper. But on the bright side, the state was gonna make us move out some old pipes, so actually he saved us a few grand in construction costs.
jokes.setup: Frank's theft being discovered
jokes.punchline: Janine finding silver lining in criminal destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that pays off Frank's original scheme while showing Janine's relentless optimism.

dialogue.speaker: Janine
dialogue.line: He must've ripped out about a hundred feet of copper. But on the bright side, the state was gonna make us move out some old pipes, so actually he saved us a few grand in construction costs.

jokes.text: I'm gonna lose my job if anyone sees this, so burn those tapes.
jokes.setup: Janine addressing the documentary crew directly
jokes.punchline: Asking them to destroy the evidence they're creating
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy that acknowledges the documentary format while creating perfect irony.

dialogue.speaker: Janine
dialogue.line: I'm gonna lose my job if anyone sees this, so burn those tapes.
